tons hay will be brought in from Min
nesota.


''To show how things have been
worked, Commissioner Root had per-
sonal friend who is big hay grower
near Rathdrum. The man said he could
supply us with plenty of hay. We or
dered from him and got reply to our
letter from Peter Morrison. Mr.. Nor
rison said we could have the hay on
board the train at Post Falls for $l7
per ton.


Fortunately, we had about three
tons of hay left over and have secured
two carloads at reasonable prices. We
have another. source of supply in mind
and are keeping our information care
fully up our sleeves.


81 asked one of the members if he
expected to keep hay at the prcsent
outrageous price, and he laughed, re-
marking that the price would take a
tumble in the spring"
